items.headline: Albemarle (ALB) is a Top-Ranked Growth Stock: Should You Buy?
items.snippet: ALB is a #2 (Buy) on the Zacks Rank, with a VGM Score of B. For fiscal 2026, five analysts revised their earnings estimate upwards in the last 60 days, and the Zacks Consensus Estimate has increased $2.69 to $13.06 per share. ALB boasts an average earnings surprise of +74.5%.

items.headline: Bull of the Day: Albemarle (ALB)
items.snippet: Albemarle (ALB) is a Zack Rank #1 (Strong Buy), the world's premier lithium producer. Q1 EPS of $2.95 crushed consensus of $1.24 by 138%. Revenue of $1.43 billion topped the $1.33 billion estimate. Adjusted EBITDA hit $664 million, up from $267.1 million a year ago. Management af

items.headline: UBS Adjusts Price Target on Albemarle to $205 From $264
items.snippet: Albemarle (ALB) has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $211.44, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

items.headline: The U.S. Is Increasing Efforts to Rebuild Its Rare Earth Supply Chain
items.snippet: Albemarle Corporation (NYSE: ALB) is the world's largest lithium producer. First-quarter 2026 adjusted EBITDA jumped 148% year over year to $664 million as lithium prices rebounded from 2025 lows. Albemarle used the recovery to cut debt too, retiring $1.3 billion of obligations d

items.headline: Lithium Is on the Rise Again. Does That Make Albemarle a Buy?
items.snippet: Shares of Albemarle (NYSE: ALB) are flat so far this year, thanks to an oversupply of lithium and a flattened demand for electric vehicles (EVs) in the United States. However, the long-term need for this critical metal is projected to increase 353% by the end of the decade. Analy

items.headline: Albemarle Likely to Continue Growing Volumes Over the Coming Years, RBC Says
items.snippet: Albemarle (ALB) is likely to continue growing volumes over the coming years from mostly brownfield at Wodgina, Atacama productivity, and opportunities at its Kings Mountain and Antofalla hubs, RBC Capital Markets said. Lithium supply is expected to remain tight through 2027. RBC
